owasp_results.description: Found 2 access control issue(s): exposed admin paths or directory listings.
owasp_results.recommendation: Restrict access to admin paths with authentication. Disable directory listing in server config.

owasp_results.description: 4 missing or misconfigured security header(s) detected.
owasp_results.recommendation: Configure your web server to send all recommended security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.).

owasp_results.description: No known outdated JavaScript libraries detected in page sources.
owasp_results.recommendation: Regularly audit third-party dependencies for known CVEs.
Use HTTPS everywhere. Remove sensitive data from HTML responses. Avoid mixed content.

findings.evidence: No TLS/SSL encryption — data transmitted in cleartext

owasp_results.recommendation: Use parameterized queries for SQL. Sanitise and encode all user input before rendering in HTML.

owasp_results.description: Found 1 page(s) exposing verbose error messages or stack traces.
owasp_results.recommendation: Configure custom error pages. Never expose stack traces or debug info in production.
Add CSRF tokens to all forms. Ensure login forms submit over HTTPS.

findings.evidence: Password field found but no CSRF token detected in the form

owasp_results.description: All external scripts include SRI attributes, or no external scripts detected.
owasp_results.recommendation: Continue using SRI for all CDN-hosted resources.

owasp_results.description: Logging and alerting configuration appears adequate.
owasp_results.recommendation: Ensure logs are monitored and alerts are configured for anomalies.

owasp_results.description: Error handling appears properly configured — no debug info leaked.
owasp_results.recommendation: Regularly test error handling paths and keep custom error pages updated.
